steps.title: Set up and recognize the vertex form approach
steps.content:
We have $s(t) = r(t) + 1 = 55t - 2t^2 + 1$, which we rewrite as $s(t) = -2t^2 + 55t + 1$.

This is a downward-opening parabola (leading coefficient is negative), so it has a maximum value.

The fastest approach is to complete the square to reach vertex form, where the maximum value is simply the constant term.

steps.title: Complete the square to find the maximum
steps.content:
Factor $-2$ from the quadratic terms:
$$s(t) = -2\left(t^2 - \frac{55}{2}t\right) + 1$$
To complete the square, add and subtract $\left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2$ inside the parentheses:
$$s(t) = -2\left[\left(t - \frac{55}{4}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2\right] + 1$$
Distribute the $-2$:
$$s(t) = -2\left(t - \frac{55}{4}\right)^2 + 2\left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2 + 1$$
Since $-2\left(t - \frac{55}{4}\right)^2 \leq 0$ for all $t$, the maximum occurs when this term equals zero:
$$1 + 2\left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
